title: [Cloud] Flamingo/Spoonbill - Connect headset as standalone
scenario: Verify Voyager Legend 50/30 standalone connection shows correct device name and earpiece-only details in Poly Studio Mobile app and cloud web page
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. Ensure no headset is currently connected. Go to Home screen and verify no device is listed.
3. Power on the Poly Voyager Legend 50 (or Voyager Legend 30) headset by pressing and holding the Power button until the LED indicator flashes.
4. Put the headset into pairing mode by pressing and holding the Bluetooth button until the LED flashes red/blue.
5. On the Android device, go to Settings → Bluetooth and pair the headset (do not use a charge case).
6. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app. Navigate to Home screen and confirm the headset appears as connected.
7. Tap on the device name to open Device Settings. Verify the device name is displayed correctly.
8. Log in to the Poly cloud web portal (e.g., Poly Lens or Poly Studio Cloud).
9. Navigate to the Devices or My Devices section and locate the paired headset.
10. Verify the cloud web page displays the device name as "Voyager Legend 50" or "Voyager Legend 30".
11. Open the device details page on the cloud web portal.
12. Verify that the details page shows only earpiece information (e.g., firmware version, battery level) and an image of just the earpiece (no charge case imagery).
expected: The Poly Voyager Legend 50 or Voyager Legend 30 headset connects as a standalone device (not via charge case). The Poly Studio Mobile app displays the device correctly. The cloud web page shows the device name as "Voyager Legend 50" or "Voyager Legend 30", and the details page displays only earpiece information and an image of just the earpiece.
